name: Martín River Route (BTT 01)
name_original: Sima de San Pedro - Cabezo de San Pedro - Ariño —  Por el Río Martín desde Alacón (BTT01)
description: Explore the Parque Cultural del Río Martín on this 22.3-mile moderate mountain biking route, featuring the Sima de San Pedro and ancient Ibe
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alacón offers routes through a rugged and varied landscape in the province of Teruel, Aragon, Spain. The region is characterized by impressive canyons and ravines, particularly within the Cultural Park of the Martín River. This area features diverse topography, including the Sierra de los Moros and the Cueva Foradada Reservoir, providing a challenging environment for off-road cycling. The terrain is suitable for mountain bikers seeking unpaved and adventurous routes. Best mountain bike trails around Alacón The most popular mountain bike trail is Iberian Route, a 22.3 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through varied terrain, offering a balanced mountain biking experience. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta al Barranco Ancho, a difficult 39.1 miles (62.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and extends through the region's dramatic canyons and ravines. Local mountain bikers also love the Martín River Route (BTT 01), a 22.3 miles (35.9 km) trail leading through the Cultural Park of the Martín River, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Alacón is defined by rugged canyons, river valleys, and diverse mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Alacón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alacón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alacón?
A: There are 11 mountain bike trails around Alacón. These routes offer a mix of moderate and difficult options, allowing riders to explore the rugged terrain of the Cultural Park of the Martín River.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for mountain biking in Alacón?
A: The terrain around Alacón is characterized by rugged canyons, dramatic ravines, and river valleys, particularly within the Cultural Park of the Martín River. You'll encounter diverse topography, including areas like the Sierra de los Moros, making for adventurous off-road cycling.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, there are 6 moderate mountain bike trails around Alacón. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999036855/iberian-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.8 km long and typically takes around 3 hours 19 minutes to complete, offering a balanced experience through varied terrain. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996649204/martin-river-route-btt-01" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín River Route (BTT 01)&lt;/a&gt;, covering 35.9 km.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Alacón?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Alacón offers 5 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006865421/vuelta-al-barranco-ancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Barranco Ancho&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 62.9 km path with significant elevation changes, winding through the region's dramatic canyons. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006847693/muniesa-huesa-del-comun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniesa - Huesa del Común -&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 61.9 km long.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Alacón?
A: Yes, there are circular mountain bike routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniesa loop from Muniesa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.5 km circular route that offers a comprehensive tour of the area's challenging terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Alacón?
A: Mountain biking in Alacón allows you to explore the stunning natural beauty of the Cultural Park of the Martín River, with its impressive canyons and ravines. You can also visit the beautiful and tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, ideal for a break by the water. The region is also known for ancient cave paintings in areas like Cerro Felio and Barranco del Mortero.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in cultural heritage. The Cultural Park of the Martín River, through which many trails pass, is renowned for its valuable cave paintings, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. You can also find religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Arcos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3635486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alacón?
A: The mountain bike routes in Alacón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alacón's varied terrain, often praising the rugged canyons, river valleys, and diverse mountain ranges that define the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Alacón?
A: Alacón's climate, typical of Teruel, Aragon, suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, especially in the canyons, making the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; a welcome spot for cooling down. Winters are generally cooler but can still offer good riding days.

Q: Are there any towns or settlements worth exploring near the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. While mountain biking, you might pass near or through charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5226391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaine&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5223985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ariño&lt;/a&gt;. These towns offer a glimpse into the local life and history of the region, providing opportunities to explore beyond the trails.

Q: What kind of elevation gain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alacón?
A: The mountain bike trails in Alacón feature significant elevation changes due to the rugged topography of canyons and mountain ranges like the Sierra de los Moros. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006865421/vuelta-al-barranco-ancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Barranco Ancho&lt;/a&gt; route involves over 1200 meters of ascent, providing a challenging workout for riders.
tips: Nice stretch of path parallel to the Martín River

The curious nature gives us this beautiful "well" 120 meters deep located to the south of the Sierra de Arcos that is undoubtedly worth a visit. Near the Sima de San Pedro we find the beautiful Iberian settlement of Cabezo de San Pedro.

Located between the towns of Oliete and Ariño, we find one of the most particular archaeological sites in Aragon due to its location on a cliff, the wall and its towers. And also very close we can visit the Sima de San Pedro.

We are in the beautiful mining town of Ariño located at the foot of the Sierra de Arcos and with many points of interest such as the Mining Museum, a spring, the parish church of San Salvador or the hermitage of Santa Bárbara among many others, so if we have time, it is definitely worth a visit.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as intermediate, making it suitable for mountain bikers with some experience. You should be comfortable handling varied surfaces, moderate climbs, and descents.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins near the Alacón wineries. While specific parking details aren't provided, Alacón is the starting point, so you'd typically find parking options within or near the village to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse landscape, starting on tracks near Alacón. The route follows the left bank of the Río Martín through poplar groves, opens up to impressive limestone cliffs, and includes river crossings and stretches of unpaved tracks. It offers a good mix of surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in natural and historical sights. Key highlights include the striking Sima de San Pedro sinkhole, the Iberian Settlement of Cabezo de San Pedro, and the San Salvador Parish Church Bell Tower. You can also find dinosaur footprints (icnitas) near Ariño and pass by a monumental juniper on the return leg.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally open, specific regulations for dogs within the Parque Cultural del Río Martín are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other visitors, and to check local park rules before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Teruel, Spain, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking. The weather is typically milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential cold or wet conditions in winter. This allows for more comfortable riding and better enjoyment of the scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Río Martín Cultural Park?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Parque Cultural del Río Martín. Generally, cultural parks in Spain do not require specific permits for trail use, nor do they have entrance fees for accessing the natural areas. However, some specific archaeological sites or caves might have separate entry fees or guided tour requirements. It's advisable to check with the park's official website for any specific regulations regarding the sites you plan to visit.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll be riding along parts of the Por el río Martín, Ruta Íbera, and Sendero del Río Martín, among others. This means you'll experience a blend of established paths within the region.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see along the Río Martín?
faqs.answer: The limestone cliffs along the Río Martín Trail are often home to numerous vultures, adding a dramatic element to the scenery. The park's diverse ecosystem also supports various other bird species and local fauna, though specific sightings can vary.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Alacón and passes through areas like Ariño. These villages typically offer basic amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. It's a good idea to check in advance for opening hours, especially in smaller towns.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 35.9 kilometers (22.4 miles) long. For an intermediate mountain biker, it typically takes around 3 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the sights.
